result.summary: Buffer exposes a GraphQL API at `https://api.buffer.com` authenticated with a Bearer personal API key, and documents OAuth/App Clients for multi-user apps; it also advertises an MCP server and a CLI, but the corpus does not reveal their connection/auth details.

buffer_api_key.label: Buffer personal API key
buffer_api_key.setup: Open the Buffer API page at [Buffer API](https://bufferapp.com/api), then in your logged-in Buffer account go to API settings and create a personal API key. Buffer says API keys are included on every plan and can be managed and rotated from API settings. Send the key as `Authorization: Bearer <your_api_key>` to `https://api.buffer.com`.

buffer_app_client.label: Buffer App Client
buffer_app_client.setup: Open [Buffer API](https://bufferapp.com/api), then in your logged-in Buffer account go to API settings and create an App Client for a multi-user app. Buffer describes this as the credential for apps where other people connect their Buffer accounts, and says OAuth is fully managed.

surfaces.name: Buffer GraphQL API
surfaces.notes: The corpus says the API is GraphQL, uses a single endpoint, and supports personal API keys for your own scripts/apps plus OAuth via App Clients for multi-user apps, but it does not provide OAuth endpoint/binding details.

surfaces.name: Buffer MCP server
surfaces.notes: Buffer advertises an MCP server with native support for Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Raycast, and Perplexity, but the corpus does not include the MCP connect URL, transport, or authentication details.

surfaces.name: Buffer CLI
surfaces.notes: The corpus mentions that 'the API and CLI treat social publishing the way you treat the rest of your stack' but does not provide the CLI command, installation package, or authentication flow.
